records.ety: [OE. war, AS. wær; akin to Icel. v<?/rr, Dan. & Sw. var, Goth. wars, G. gewahr aware, OHG. wara notice, attention, Gr. <?/ to see. Cf. Aware, Garment, Garnish, Garrison, Panorama, Ward, v. t. Ware, a., Warren.]

defs: Cautious of danger; carefully watching and guarding against deception, artifices, and dangers; timorously or suspiciously prudent; circumspect; scrupulous; careful.
quotes: We should be wary, therefore, what persecution we raise against the living labors of public men.
It behoveth our words to be wary and few.
